readiness.summary: The baseline is accepted for specification with explicit non-material gaps for production topology, recovery targets, and operations ownership.

narrative.executive_summary: Team invitations and role-based authorization remain in one Python application runtime with explicit authorization, invitation, persistence, trust, and transaction boundaries.
scope: Architecture-level component, deployment, data, trust, contract, transition, quality, and operations consequences
non_goals: Endpoint payload, database schema, or implementation-test specification
narrative.architecture_overview: A browser or API client calls the invitation component over HTTPS. The invitation component crosses the in-process authorization boundary before PostgreSQL persistence, and invitation acceptance coordinates token consumption with unique membership creation in one transaction.

assumptions.statement: The existing Python runtime and PostgreSQL database remain adequate for the recorded invitation load.

validation_strategy.statement: Verify authorization-before-write, idempotent acceptance, literal latency criteria, and deterministic architecture traceability before downstream implementation approval.

narrative.evidence_boundary: The baseline uses the written plan, accepted direction, brief, ADR, Python fixture, Dockerfile, and local Docker Compose topology. No production deployment description, recovery objective, or operations ownership record was available.
narrative.consistency_model: Invitation acceptance consumes one token and creates at most one team membership through a transaction and uniqueness invariant; authorization denial terminates before a durable write.
narrative.security_privacy_summary: The architecture realizes both plan-owned threat obligations through a public API boundary and an explicit authorization chokepoint. This is design traceability, not security acceptance.
narrative.operability_summary: The request path exposes latency, denial, persistence-failure, and replay signals, while production signal ownership and runbook ownership remain GAP-003.
narrative.capacity_resilience_recovery_summary: The brief records a p95 latency requirement and the fixture records one local application and database. Production capacity, topology, availability, and recovery targets remain explicit downstream gaps.
narrative.cost_complexity_summary: The selected single-runtime direction avoids a new service boundary and migration while retaining logical seams for a later evidence-backed extraction.

drivers.name: Existing runtime fit
drivers.statement: The capability extends the repository's existing Python service and PostgreSQL deployment.
drivers.consequence: Authorization, invitation, and persistence responsibilities remain logical components in one deployable runtime.

drivers.name: Authorization before persistence
drivers.statement: Protected invitation and membership writes require authorization before persistence.
drivers.consequence: Every protected write crosses the authorization component before the PostgreSQL write path.

drivers.name: Idempotent invitation acceptance
drivers.statement: Invitation acceptance is idempotent per invitation token.
drivers.consequence: Token consumption and unique membership creation share one transactional contract.

drivers.name: Invitation latency target
drivers.statement: Invitation creation must complete at p95 under 500 ms under normal load.
drivers.consequence: The synchronous request path remains bounded and observable.

drivers.name: Recorded local topology
drivers.statement: The repository records a Docker Compose application plus PostgreSQL topology.
drivers.consequence: The baseline describes the observed local topology without claiming an unrecorded production topology.

actors.name: Team administrator
roles: Choose an existing team role for an invitee

actors.name: Invited user
roles: Accept an invitation token and become a team member

system_boundary.name: Team invitations and RBAC system
system_boundary.responsibility: Authorize team operations and manage invitation and membership lifecycle within the written plan.
in_scope: Invitation creation, listing, acceptance, and membership creation

context_relationships.interaction: Accept an invitation and become a team member

components.name: Browser or API client
responsibilities: Submit invitation and acceptance requests and present explicit outcomes

components.name: Invitation application component
responsibilities: Coordinate invitation creation, listing, token validation, and acceptance

components.name: Authorization and membership component
responsibilities: Own membership invariants and authorization-before-write behavior

components.name: PostgreSQL data store
responsibilities: Persist invitation and membership records as the source of truth

relationships.interaction: Submit authenticated invitation and acceptance requests
Evaluate authorization and coordinate membership behavior
Persist and read invitation state within the acceptance transaction
Read and write membership state with uniqueness enforcement

deployments.replica_strategy: Client instances are caller-managed; production expectations are tracked by GAP-001.
deployments.scaling: Client concurrency is outside the deployable application boundary.
deployments.failover: Caller retry is bounded by the interaction contract and explicit API outcomes.

deployments.replica_strategy: The fixture records one local application service; production replica strategy is tracked by GAP-001.
deployments.scaling: Production application scaling is not recorded and is tracked by GAP-001.
deployments.failover: Production application failover is not recorded and is tracked by GAP-001.

deployments.replica_strategy: Authorization shares the application placement and its GAP-001 production replica decision.
deployments.scaling: Authorization scales with the application placement under the future production topology.
deployments.failover: Authorization shares the application failure domain until a new plan and ADR change it.

deployments.replica_strategy: The fixture records one local database service; production replica strategy is tracked by GAP-001.
deployments.scaling: Production database scaling is not recorded and is tracked by GAP-001.
deployments.failover: Production database failover is not recorded and is tracked by GAP-001.

deployment_connections.purpose: Submit invitation and acceptance requests and receive explicit outcomes
deployment_connections.network_boundary: Public client to application boundary; production controls are tracked by GAP-001.

deployment_connections.purpose: Read and write invitation and membership state transactionally
deployment_connections.network_boundary: Local Compose application-to-data boundary; production controls are tracked by GAP-001.

data_entities.name: membership
data_entities.consistency: Membership creation is unique per team and user and is coordinated transactionally with invitation acceptance.
data_entities.region_residency: Production residency is not recorded; GAP-001 owns closure.
data_entities.backup_recovery: Production backup and recovery targets are not recorded; GAP-002 owns closure.

data_entities.name: invitation
data_entities.consistency: Invitation token consumption and membership creation complete in one acceptance transaction.
data_entities.region_residency: Production residency is not recorded; GAP-001 owns closure.
data_entities.backup_recovery: Production backup and recovery targets are not recorded; GAP-002 owns closure.

integration_flows.name: Invitation API request
integration_flows.failure_behavior: The caller receives an explicit HTTP failure and no success is implied.
integration_flows.timeout_retry: A caller may retry only under the invitation-token idempotency behavior realized by CTR-001.
integration_flows.details: The public request carries team, email, role, or token input into the application boundary.

integration_flows.name: Authorization and membership call
integration_flows.failure_behavior: Authorization denial stops the request before any invitation or membership persistence.
integration_flows.timeout_retry: The in-process result is returned in the same request; callers do not bypass a denial.
integration_flows.details: The logical component boundary is explicit even though both responsibilities share one runtime.

integration_flows.name: Invitation and membership transaction
integration_flows.failure_behavior: The transaction rolls back; replay returns the existing membership without duplication.
integration_flows.timeout_retry: Retry is safe only through the idempotent token and unique membership contract.
integration_flows.details: The existing PostgreSQL store is the source of truth for both durable nouns.

dynamic_scenarios.name: Authorize, create, and accept an invitation
dynamic_scenarios.trigger: A team administrator invites a person and the recipient later accepts the issued token.
dynamic_scenarios.success_outcome: An authorized invitation is persisted and one idempotent acceptance creates exactly one team membership.

steps.failure_behavior: The client presents an explicit validation or authorization failure.
An HTTP failure returns without implying invitation creation.
A denial stops the scenario before persistence.
The transaction rolls back and the caller receives an explicit failure.

steps.interaction: Submit the invitation token for acceptance
steps.failure_behavior: An invalid token produces an explicit failure without membership creation.
The response distinguishes failure from an accepted result.

steps.interaction: Authorize membership creation and enforce membership invariants
steps.failure_behavior: A denial or membership-invariant failure stops acceptance before persistence.

steps.interaction: Consume the invitation and create membership transactionally
steps.failure_behavior: Rollback preserves the prior durable state; replay returns the existing membership.

alternate_paths.name: Authorization denied
alternate_paths.condition: The caller lacks the authorization required for the attempted invitation or membership write.
alternate_paths.outcome: Return denial and create no invitation or membership.

alternate_paths.name: Acceptance replay
alternate_paths.condition: The invitation token was already accepted.
alternate_paths.outcome: Return the existing membership without creating a duplicate.

trust_boundaries.name: Public API boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Untrusted invitation and bearer-token input crosses from a user-controlled client into the application.
trust_boundaries.residency: Production processing residency is tracked by GAP-001.

trust_boundaries.name: Authorization chokepoint
trust_boundaries.description: Invitation orchestration crosses the central capability check before protected persistence.
trust_boundaries.residency: Logical in-process boundary within the selected single application runtime.

tactics: Evaluate the required team capability before protected persistence
Stop on denial without writing invitation or membership state
security_realizations.verification: Automated capability-denial assertions prove HTTP 403 and no durable write.
Automated validation, redaction, denial, and no-write assertions cover the invitation boundary.

contract_realizations.behavior: Invitation acceptance consumes one token and creates at most one membership per team and user.
contract_realizations.failure_behavior: A replay returns the existing membership, while a failed transaction creates neither a partial acceptance nor a duplicate.
contract_realizations.compatibility: The plan-owned IC-001 remains authoritative for both feature specifications and any later evolution.
contract_realizations.verification: Replay, uniqueness, rollback, and concurrent-acceptance tests after specification.

quality_scenarios.name: Invitation creation latency
quality_scenarios.stimulus: A team administrator submits a valid invitation.
quality_scenarios.tactic: Keep authorization and persistence in a bounded synchronous path and use indexed PostgreSQL access.
quality_scenarios.verification: /core-engineering:ce-probe-perf against the accepted criterion after implementation.
quality_scenarios.details: The target is a written requirement, not measured performance evidence.

quality_scenarios.name: Unauthorized invitation denial
quality_scenarios.stimulus: An unauthorized caller submits an invitation.
quality_scenarios.response: The request is denied before invitation persistence.
quality_scenarios.target: Unauthorized invitation attempts return HTTP 403 and create no invitation.
quality_scenarios.tactic: Cross SR-001 before the PostgreSQL invitation write.
quality_scenarios.verification: Automated HTTP response and no-persistence assertions.
quality_scenarios.details: The scenario binds the threat obligation to an observable no-write result.

quality_scenarios.name: Idempotent invitation acceptance
quality_scenarios.stimulus: A recipient or client retries invitation acceptance.
quality_scenarios.environment: normal operation, timeout recovery, or concurrent retry
quality_scenarios.response: The existing membership is returned without duplication.
quality_scenarios.target: Invitation acceptance is idempotent per invitation token.
quality_scenarios.tactic: Consume the token and create membership transactionally with a uniqueness invariant.
quality_scenarios.verification: Automated replay, rollback, uniqueness, and concurrency tests.
quality_scenarios.details: IC-001 supplies the architecture-level invariant; specification owns concrete schema and test cases.

operations.name: Invitation and authorization path health
operations.responsibility: Detect invitation latency, authorization denials, persistence failures, and unsafe token exposure.
operations.failure_domain: single application request and PostgreSQL transaction path
operations.target: Use QA-001, QA-002, and QA-003 as architecture thresholds and behavioral outcomes.
operations.tactic: Emit correlated metrics and structured failures without invitation-token values.
operations.verification: Review the telemetry contract during specification and probe the implemented path during verification.

operations.name: Invitation and membership recovery
operations.responsibility: Recover invitation and membership state without violating authorization or idempotency invariants.
operations.failure_domain: PostgreSQL durable state and the shared application runtime
operations.tactic: Preserve transactional rollback and uniqueness; define production recovery objectives before deployment.
operations.verification: Exercise rollback and replay behavior after implementation, then verify the accepted recovery target before deployment.

direction_realizations.statement: Authorization owns capability checks; invitations owns invitation lifecycle.
One application runtime with the existing PostgreSQL data store.
Invitation and membership writes remain explicit and transactionally coordinated.
In-process authorization failure stops before persistence.
Every protected write crosses the authorization boundary first.
Transactional acceptance provides idempotent membership creation.
No runtime migration; logical boundaries can later be extracted if evidence requires it.
C01 decomposes into authorization foundation followed by invitation delivery.
The existing runtime and database remain adequate for the recorded load.
No irreversible infrastructure commitment is introduced.

decisions.title: Single application runtime
decisions.context: Authorization and invitation features share a Python service, PostgreSQL state, protected writes, and one transaction boundary.
decisions.decision: Keep authorization, membership, and invitation responsibilities in one application runtime with explicit logical components and a shared PostgreSQL data store.
decisions.rationale: The direction satisfies authorization-before-persistence and idempotency without adding a runtime or network boundary.

alternatives.option: Separate invitation runtime with eventual authorization
alternatives.consequence: Adds a service, trust, protocol, deployment, and reconciliation boundary.
alternatives.rejection_reason: It permits pending invitation persistence before the mandatory authorization decision.

consequences: Authorization and invitations share one deployment blast radius.
A later service extraction requires a new plan, ADR, and transition.
Verification covers the shared transaction and protected-write boundary.
decisions.reversibility: Logical seams preserve a future extraction path, but a split requires an explicit transition plan.
decisions.cost_if_wrong: Cross-feature runtime, data-ownership, and operational rework.

open_questions.question: What production provider, region, zone, network, residency, scaling, and availability topology will host the application and PostgreSQL?
options: Retain a production-equivalent single-runtime container topology
Select a managed application and PostgreSQL topology through a separately approved deployment decision

open_questions.question: What recovery objective and backup policy apply to invitation and membership state?
options: Adopt an existing documented PostgreSQL recovery policy
Record project-specific RPO and RTO targets before deployment

open_questions.question: Which human-owned operations role owns production signals and runbooks for the invitation and membership path?

risks.title: Local trust authentication
risks.statement: The fixture's PostgreSQL trust authentication is suitable only for local evaluation.
risks.impact: Reusing the local setting in production would weaken database access control.
risks.mitigation: Require managed credentials and a reviewed production network policy before deployment.
risks.contingency: Block production deployment until non-trust authentication is evidenced.
risks.trigger: A deployment proposal carries POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD=trust beyond local evaluation.

risks.title: Future notification boundary
risks.statement: A future email delivery adapter would introduce an integration and trust boundary outside this fixture.
risks.impact: Unplanned delivery coupling could change failure, privacy, and retry behavior.
risks.mitigation: Revise the plan and architecture before adding email delivery.
risks.contingency: Keep the token-returning test harness as the bounded fixture behavior.
risks.trigger: The product scope adds outbound notification delivery.

risks.title: Runtime and database adequacy
risks.statement: The selected direction assumes the existing runtime and database remain adequate for the recorded load.
risks.impact: Unexpected load could breach the latency target or force a topology revision.
risks.mitigation: Verify QA-001 against representative load before release and revise the architecture if the target is missed.
risks.contingency: Scale or revise the single-runtime topology through an approved follow-up decision.
risks.trigger: Representative performance evidence misses p95 under 500 ms.

gaps.statement: Production provider, region, zones, network zones, residency, scaling, availability, replica, and failover topology are not recorded.
gaps.impact: Deployment and residency controls cannot be approved from the local Compose fixture.
gaps.next_action: Select and record the production topology before deployment.
gaps.closure_criteria: An accepted deployment source replaces every production topology unknown and updates the affected nodes, placements, connections, and trust-boundary residency.

gaps.statement: Production backup policy, recovery point objective, and recovery time objective are not recorded.
gaps.impact: Recovery adequacy for invitation and membership state cannot be verified before deployment.
gaps.next_action: Record the applicable recovery and backup targets before deployment.
gaps.closure_criteria: An accepted source defines the targets and OP-002 plus both data entities record their recovery behavior.

gaps.statement: Production operations ownership and runbook ownership are not recorded.
gaps.impact: Invitation-path alerts or recovery work could lack an accountable responder.
gaps.next_action: Assign a human-owned operations role and record the production runbook authority before implementation.
gaps.closure_criteria: An accepted source names the owner and OP-001 plus OP-002 replace their ownership and runbook unknowns.
